title: 12 Dwarf White Daffodil Bulbs – Mixed Spring Flowering Bulbs with Pure White Blooms and Delightful Fragrance – Cultivated for UK Climate Conditions
features: Ideal Planting Time: Plant these bulbs from August to December, and you'll see their beautiful flowers bloom between February and April. These dwarf daffodils, standing at 20-25 cm tall, bring a lovely scent to your garden in early to mid-spring. They are perfect for both novice and experienced gardeners and are specifically cultivated for the UK climate.

Easy Planting Instructions: Planting these bulbs is simple! Just plant them 10 cm deep and 10 cm apart. You'll soon enjoy a landscape adorned with beautiful flowers. These bulbs thrive in well-drained, acidic soil—whether clay, loamy, or sandy. They need full sun but can also grow well in partial shade.
What's Included: You'll receive 12 Dwarf Daffodil White Scented Mix bulbs. These Narcissus bulbs are 8/10 in size and are ready to bring a delightful fragrance to your garden.

Beautify Your Garden: Get creative and enjoy months of fragrant white daffodils by growing your own. This mix includes daffodils with various cup sizes, shapes, and hues, perfect for naturalized areas, borders, and garden beds. They will extend your blooming season and make your garden look stunning.

We've got your back! When you get these bulbs from us, they're guaranteed to arrive in primo condition. We've been in cahoots with our suppliers for years to ensure you get nothing but the best. We know the disappointment of eagerly waiting for something to bloom, only to be let down. With us, you're getting top-notch, healthy bulbs, no surprises there!
description: Product Description Transform Your Garden with Dwarf Daffodil White Scented Mix Stunning Spring Blooms Perfect Planting Time : For the best results, plant your bulbs between August and December. These stunning daffodils will bloom from February to April, adding a touch of elegance and fragrance to your garden in early to mid-spring. With a height of 20-25 cm, these spring flowering bulbs are a must-have for any garden enthusiast. Ideal for All Gardeners : Whether you're an experienced gardener or a beginner, these bulbs are cultivated for UK climate conditions, making the planting process relaxed and stress-free. Effortless Planting Instructions Simple Steps to Success Effortless Planting : Planting these bulbs is as easy as 1-2-3! Simply plant them 10 cm deep and 10 cm apart. You'll be rewarded with irresistible flowers that will enhance your landscape. Optimal Soil and Sunlight Requirements : These bulbs thrive in well-drained, acidic soil, whether it’s clay, loamy, or sandy. They prefer full sun but can also grow well in partial shade. With these simple requirements, you can ensure a healthy and vibrant display. What’s Inside Your Package Quality Bulbs : Each package contains 12 Dwarf Daffodil White Scented Mix bulbs. These Narcissus bulbs are size 8/10, carefully selected to provide you with a stunning display of white blooms that will fill your garden with a delightful fragrance. Beautify Your Garden Express Your Creativity Express Your Creativity : Show off your gardening skills and get rewarded with months of fragrant white daffodils. The Dwarf Daffodil White Scented Mix features cups of various sizes, shapes, and hues, perfect for adding diversity to your garden. Versatile Planting Options : These bulbs are ideal for naturalized areas, borders, and garden beds. By planting these daffodils, you’ll extend your blooming season and create a beautiful, eye-catching garden. Versatile Garden Enhancements Borders and Pathways : Dwarf daffodils make excellent border plants. Their uniform height and prolific blooming can create a striking outline for garden beds and pathways. The pure white blooms add a classic and timeless touch to any garden design. Containers and Pots : For those with limited garden space or urban dwellers with balconies, planting daffodils in containers is an excellent option. These bulbs thrive in pots and bring life to patios, balconies, and window sills. Naturalized Areas : If you prefer a more natural and less structured garden look, these bulbs are perfect for naturalizing. Scatter them in lawns or under trees, and they will come back year after year, gradually increasing in number and creating a beautiful, wildflower-like display. Experience the Joy of Gardening Planting the Dwarf Daffodil White Scented Mix is a rewarding experience. Imagine your garden filled with the pure white blooms and irresistible fragrance of these daffodils. With their easy planting process and minimal maintenance, you’ll find joy in watching your garden transform. These bulbs are cultivated to thrive in the UK climate, ensuring a beautiful and successful garden every spring. Technical Guide: Planting Dwarf Daffodil White Scented Bulbs 1. Preparation Materials Needed: Dwarf Daffodil White Scented Bulbs Garden trowel or bulb planter Measuring tape or ruler Compost or organic matter (optional) Watering can or garden hose Mulch (optional) Site Selection: Choose a location that receives full sun to partial shade. Ensure the soil is well-drained to prevent bulb rot. If your soil is heavy clay, consider amending it with compost or organic matter to improve drainage. 2. Planting Procedure Timing: Plant bulbs between August and December for optimal spring blooming. Soil Preparation: Loosen the soil to a depth of 30 cm (12 inches) using a garden fork or tiller. Incorporate compost or organic matter into the soil to enhance fertility and drainage. Planting Depth and Spacing: Dig holes 10 cm (4 inches) deep and 10 cm (4 inches) apart using a garden trowel or bulb planter. Use a measuring tape or ruler to ensure accurate depth and spacing. Planting the Bulbs: Place each bulb in the hole with the pointed end facing upward. Cover the bulbs with soil, gently firming it down to eliminate air pockets. Watering: Water the planted area thoroughly to help settle the soil and initiate root growth. Maintain moderate soil moisture during the growing season but avoid over-watering. Mulching: Apply a 5 cm (2 inch) layer of mulch over the planted area to conserve moisture, regulate soil temperature, and reduce weed growth. 3. Maintenance Watering: During the growing season, keep the soil consistently moist but not waterlogged. Reduce watering once the foliage starts to die back naturally after blooming. Fertilizing: Apply a balanced, slow-release fertilizer at planting time and again in early spring as the shoots emerge. Follow the manufacturer's instructions for application rates and methods. Post-Bloom Care: Allow the foliage to die back naturally, as this process enables the bulbs to store energy for the next blooming cycle. Avoid cutting back the leaves until they are fully yellowed and withered. Pest and Disease Management: Monitor for common pests such as slugs and snails, and use appropriate control measures if necessary. Ensure good air circulation around the plants to prevent fungal diseases. Remove any diseased or damaged foliage promptly. 4. Special Considerations Soil pH: Dwarf daffodils prefer slightly acidic to neutral soil (pH 6.0-7.0). Test your soil pH and amend if necessary. Replanting and Division: Over time, daffodil bulbs can become crowded. Every 3-5 years, lift and divide the bulbs after the foliage has died back. Replant the divided bulbs immediately, following the same planting depth and spacing guidelines. 5. Troubleshooting Poor Blooming: Ensure bulbs are planted at the correct depth and spacing. Check for adequate sunlight and well-drained soil. Avoid over-fertilizing, as excessive nitrogen can inhibit blooming. Bulb Rot: Ensure the planting site has good drainage. Avoid over-watering and amend heavy soils with organic matter. By following these technical guidelines, you can successfully plant and maintain Dwarf Daffodil White Scented Bulbs, ensuring a beautiful and fragrant addition to your garden each spring.
